concepts.name: Decision
concepts.description: A recorded decision under decisions/YYYY/MM/DD/<id>.decision.riido.json. Conforms to decisions/schema.riido.json.
examples: 001-initial-agreement

concepts.name: ConceptMap
concepts.description: This file. Captures relationships and prose constraints that DSL cannot yet express.
examples: concept-map/concept-map.riido.json

concepts.name: SsotDependencyMap
concepts.description: Declares which repos consume which SSOT artifacts and which generated artifacts depend on which sources. Used by tools/ssotdeps verify.
examples: ssot-dependency-map.riido.json

concepts.name: DevPatternEvent
concepts.description: Recorded selection of top_down vs bottom_up for a work item, written as JSONL by tools/devpattern.
examples: {"work_item":"WI-001","source":"top_down","at":"2026-06-03T..."}

concepts.name: WorkItem
concepts.description: Unit of work an agent picks up. SSOT is dsl/domain/work-item.lisp; emitted to ir/domain/work-item.ir.json; consumed by backend and frontend through generated clients (never re-declared).
examples: {"id":"WI-001","title":"Bootstrap contracts repo","state":"done"}

concepts.name: WorkItemCreated
concepts.description: Emitted when a new WorkItem appears. SSOT is dsl/domain/work-item-created.lisp; emitted to ir/domain/work-item-created.ir.json. Backend SSE endpoint /events emits this as its only event type in decision 003's scope.
examples: {"work_item_id":"WI-001","title":"Bootstrap contracts repo"}

concepts.name: GeneratedClient
concepts.description: Per-language consumer-side files emitted from IR by tools/gen-go-client and tools/gen-dart-client. Committed into the consumer repo; never edited by hand; drift detected by per-repo CI gates.
examples: backend/internal/contracts/work_item.go
frontend/lib/contracts_client/work_item.dart

concepts.name: ProbeBaseline
concepts.description: Output of `probe preflight`: the aggregate green/red state of every contracts-local verifier (decision validate, ssotdeps verify --mode local, conceptmap verify, secretscan, irdrift). status=candidate_allowed means the dumb-agent may create one candidate; status=baseline_blocked means stop.
examples: {"status":"candidate_allowed","candidate_allowed":true,"next_allowed_action":"create-candidate"}

concepts.name: EnvironmentBlocker
concepts.description: A reports/environment-blockers/<id>.md note describing an environment or setup issue (missing tool, broken baseline, missing sibling artifact). Filed by dumb-agent under a red baseline as the only allowed write. NOT a domain guard gap.
examples: reports/environment-blockers/eb-001-sbcl-missing.md

concepts.name: Fixture
concepts.description: A .json file under fixtures/positive/ or fixtures/negative/ with a .meta.json sidecar declaring fixture_type, expected (pass|fail), and optionally expected_error_contains. Walked by `probe fixtures`. v1 supports fixture_type=decision only.
examples: fixtures/positive/decision/min.json + min.meta.json

concepts.name: GraphQLQuery
concepts.description: Read-only GraphQL field defined by defquery in DSL. Its wire-name and returns-shape are SSOT; backend resolvers and frontend clients reference them via generated constants (Go: <Name>QueryName + <Name>Result; Dart: <camelName>QueryName). The hand-written GraphQL parser+executor remains hand-written until a separate library decision.
examples: defquery list-work-items :wire-name "workItems" :returns-list work-item

concepts.name: IRSchema
concepts.description: JSON Schema (ir/schema/ir.schema.json) describing the structural shape of every ir/domain/<name>.ir.json document. Decision 007 added this; before then, C-002 referenced a schema that didn't exist. probe fixtures mirrors these rules in Go (no third-party validator dep) so fixture_type=ir-aggregate / ir-event / query can be checked.
examples: ir/schema/ir.schema.json
fixtures/positive/work-item/min.json with fixture_type ir-aggregate

concepts.name: MergeAuthority
concepts.description: Bounded merge grant attached to a role (currently only dumb-agent). agentguard merge-check decides merge_allowed iff every changed file matches auto_merge_paths, none matches forbidden_paths, file count <= max_files_per_pr. Stricter than write-time agentguard verify which uses allowed_paths.
examples: dumb-agent merge_authority.granted=true with auto_merge_paths covering fixtures/positive, fixtures/negative, fuzz/corpus, reports/guard-candidates, reports/environment-blockers

relationships.example: 001-initial-agreement introduced the concept-map artifact and its schema.
Each concept/relationship/constraint optionally cites the decision that introduced it.
A decision claiming generated_artifacts must list a source whose path is declared in ssot-dependency-map.riido.json.
Creating a new WorkItem emits one WorkItemCreated event on the backend SSE surface.
backend/internal/contracts/work_item.go is the Go representation of ir/domain/work-item.ir.json; frontend/lib/contracts_client/work_item.dart is the Dart representation.
If ProbeBaseline.status is baseline_blocked, no new Fixture (positive, negative, or fuzz) may be created in that PR. Only one EnvironmentBlocker may be filed.
Every environment-blocker report cites the probe preflight output that justified filing it.
list-work-items returns a list of WorkItem; the returns.type in IR points to the aggregate kebab-name.
backend/internal/contracts/list_work_items.go exposes ListWorkItemsQueryName and ListWorkItemsResult; frontend/lib/contracts_client/list_work_items.dart exposes listWorkItemsQueryName.

constraints.rule: No repo other than agent-cluster-contracts may define a domain vocabulary term (aggregate, entity, value-object, event, policy, lifecycle state, view).
constraints.rationale: Vocabulary is the SSOT for cross-repo communication. If backend or frontend defines its own, the same concept ends up with two names and contracts drift silently.
examples: backend writes `type WorkItem struct {...}` imported from generated code emitted by ir/domain/work-item.ir.json — OK.
frontend reads `WorkItemView` from the generated client — OK.
counterexamples: backend declares `type Task struct {...}` ad-hoc to mean what contracts calls WorkItem — NOT OK.
frontend renames `WorkItem` to `Job` in UI state — NOT OK.

guard_candidate.tool: vocablint (D014) — scans for type/class declarations matching IR aggregate/event names outside generated client paths. Runs in contracts.yml and in each consumer repo's contracts-drift.yml.

constraints.rule: Every JSON IR file must declare its DSL source file path and a sha256 of that source. Mismatch fails CI. The structural shape of every IR file is defined by ir/schema/ir.schema.json (mirrored in code by internal/probe.validateIRDoc).
constraints.rationale: IR is generated. Without a source pin, hand-edits are undetectable and downstream consumers lock in drift. Without a structural schema, codegen and consumers cannot rely on shape invariants.
examples: ir/domain/work-item.ir.json contains {"source":{"dsl_file":"dsl/domain/work-item.lisp","sha256":"..."}} and conforms to ir/schema/ir.schema.json (kind=aggregate branch).
counterexamples: An IR file missing the `source` block.
IR sha256 not matching the current DSL file.

guard_candidate.tool: irdrift (D003) re-emits IR from DSL via SBCL and diffs against committed ir/domain/*.ir.json — catches missing source field, wrong dsl_file path, or stale sha256. ir/schema/ir.schema.json (D007) requires the source object structurally. Both run in contracts.yml.

constraints.rule: No file committed to any of the three repos may contain a raw secret matching a known pattern (AWS key, GitHub token, JWT, PEM, etc.).
constraints.rationale: Secrets in git history are unrecoverable. Even after rotation, the trust boundary has already been crossed.
examples: .github/workflows/*.yml references ${{ secrets.SLACK_BOT_TOKEN }} — OK.
Documentation states 'requires GitHub Secret named SLACK_BOT_TOKEN' — OK.
counterexamples: A fixture file containing `xoxb-...` — NOT OK.
A README pasting a `gho_...` token to show example usage — NOT OK.

constraints.rule: Every work item recorded by an agent must declare its source pattern as either top_down or bottom_up before implementation begins.
constraints.rationale: If we can't see which work was reactive vs planned, we can't later evaluate whether random selection is harmful or which kinds of bugs come from which pattern.
examples: Agent runs `tools/devpattern select --work-item WI-014` and gets `top_down`; the JSONL event is written before code changes.
counterexamples: Agent starts editing files for WI-014 with no devpattern event recorded.

guard_candidate.tool: github-action: verify devpattern event per WI in PR

constraints.rule: A dumb-agent must not create any Fixture (positive, negative, or fuzz) or domain guard-candidate report when ProbeBaseline.status is baseline_blocked. The only allowed write under a red baseline is at most one EnvironmentBlocker report.
constraints.rationale: Under a red baseline, validators are already failing on the trunk. A new failing candidate proves nothing about whether a verifier is too loose — it could just be the trunk being broken. Allowing fixture creation in this state produces noise that looks like guard gaps but isn't.
examples: probe preflight returns status=baseline_blocked. Dumb-agent files reports/environment-blockers/eb-001-sbcl-missing.md describing the broken baseline and stops. No fixture changes.
counterexamples: probe preflight returns status=baseline_blocked. Dumb-agent files a fixtures/negative/work-item/x.json claiming the verifier missed a case — the verifier did not miss anything; the baseline was simply broken.

guard_candidate.tool: probe preflight (already enforced for dumb-agent via AGENT_CONTRACT.md; future hook: pre-commit refusing fixture writes when probe preflight is red)

constraints.rule: A role may auto-merge a PR only if every changed file matches at least one auto_merge_paths glob declared on that role, no file matches any forbidden_paths glob, and the file count does not exceed max_files_per_pr. agentguard merge-check is the executable predicate.
constraints.rationale: Bounded merge authority lets dumb-agent close its own loop (write candidate → CI → merge) without granting it the power to alter guard rails. Restricting merges to auto_merge_paths (a subset of allowed_paths) keeps writable-but-needs-review classes — like ir/candidates/ — out of the auto-merge surface.
examples: dumb-agent opens a PR adding fixtures/positive/x.json + .meta.json. merge-check returns merge_allowed. CI passes. Dumb-agent calls gh pr merge.
dumb-agent opens a PR adding ir/candidates/exp.json. merge-check returns merge_blocked: 'not auto-mergeable: outside auto_merge_paths'. Designer reviews and merges manually.
counterexamples: dumb-agent opens a PR editing tools/decision/main.go and tries to auto-merge — merge-check FORBIDDEN, never gets past.
A role with auto_merge_eligible: true but no auto_merge_paths declared — merge-check refuses everything (defensive default).

guard_candidate.tool: agentguard merge-check (D010 ships predicate) + .github/workflows/dumb-agent-automerge.yml (D012 ships executor — gh pr merge --auto on merge_allowed PRs)

constraints.rule: Backend and frontend must never hand-type a GraphQL query/mutation/subscription wire identifier or result shape. They reference the generated constants emitted by gen-go-client / gen-dart-client from kind=query IR (and future kind=mutation, kind=subscription IR).
constraints.rationale: GraphQL wire identifiers are vocabulary. Per C-001 vocabulary is contracts-owned and never re-declared by consumers. Hand-typing "workItems" in backend would mean the contracts repo can rename the query but the backend would silently keep the old wire identifier — exactly the drift the SSOT chain is supposed to prevent.
examples: backend/internal/slice/server.go uses contracts.ListWorkItemsQueryName instead of the literal "workItems".
frontend Dart client imports listWorkItemsQueryName from generated lib/contracts_client/list_work_items.dart.
counterexamples: backend writes const supportedQuery = "{ workItems { id title state } }" with "workItems" as a literal — the wire identifier is duplicated outside SSOT and will drift.

frontend hardcodes the wire literal in a GraphQL document string — same drift class. (Concrete wire name elided here so wirelint does not flag this counterexample; constraint C-006 applies to any IR-declared wire name.)

guard_candidate.tool: wirelint (tools/wirelint) — scans for quoted literal occurrences of any kind=query wire_name outside generated-client paths. Runs in contracts.yml and in each consumer repo's contracts-drift.yml.

constraints.rule: Every fixture .meta.json must declare a non-empty `purpose` field. No two fixtures sharing the same (category, fixture_type) triple may share the same purpose value. Probe fixtures rejects missing purpose AND duplicate-purpose entries.
constraints.rationale: Without purpose+dedup, an agent (notably dumb-agent in cycle mode) can flood fixtures/ with structurally identical entries that differ only by id/title/timestamp. They pass the verifier — but they test nothing new. C-008 forces each fixture to declare WHY it is novel and rejects clones, shaping the agent toward coverage growth instead of noise.
examples: fixtures/positive/decision/decision-valid-minimal.meta.json declares purpose='decision valid minimal' — clear, unique within (positive, decision).
fixtures/negative/decision/decision-invalid-missing-evidence.meta.json declares purpose='decision invalid missing evidence' — distinct from the missing-owner / missing-status / missing-title cousins.
counterexamples: Pre-D015 noise: 19 decision-positive-cycle-<timestamp>.json fixtures with no purpose field and identical content. After D015 they're rejected.
Two negative fixtures both declaring purpose='catches a parse error' — only one is allowed; the second is a duplicate even if the JSON content differs.

guard_candidate.tool: probe fixtures (D015): reads meta.purpose, fails on empty or duplicate (category, fixture_type, purpose) triples

constraints.rule: GitHub branch protection on main must have strict_required_status_checks_policy=true. A PR cannot merge until its base is up-to-date with main AND its required checks re-pass against that updated base.
constraints.rationale: Without strict=true, a PR can pass CI under old verifier code, then merge after a stricter verifier lands on main — bringing files into main that would fail under main's current verifier. Observed at D015: PR #65 (cycle-30 fixture) merged after D015's purpose requirement landed, even though the fixture lacked purpose. strict=true closes that race window for every new gate.
examples: Repo ruleset 17209686 ('main' ruleset) has rules[type=required_status_checks].parameters.strict_required_status_checks_policy=true. After this decision, dumb-agent automerge requires PRs to be rebased to current main before gh pr merge --auto fires.
When the contracts repo adds a new required check (e.g. vocablint becomes a required status), every queued PR must rebase + repass before merging.
counterexamples: strict=false (pre-D016): PR opened at SHA X, CI runs against X+base, passes; D015 lands at SHA Y > X; PR merges into Y carrying file that fails the Y verifier. Main goes red.
Disabling the strict policy on a future ruleset edit — explicitly forbidden without superseding D016.

guard_candidate.tool: gh api repos/.../rulesets/17209686 PUT with strict_required_status_checks_policy=true (configured 2026-06-03). Manual change reversible via repo settings — a future check could query the API and fail CI if strict ever goes back to false.

constraints.rule: Fixture purpose dedup compares NormalizePurpose(purpose), not raw purpose. NormalizePurpose strips parenthesized digit runs, 'cycle N' tokens, and bare 6+-digit numbers (likely Unix timestamps), then collapses whitespace and lowercases. Two fixtures whose purposes normalize to the same string are duplicates even if the raw strings differ.
constraints.rationale: D015 required non-empty unique purpose. Observed within 24h: dumb-agent adapted by appending Unix timestamps to a template purpose ('Ensure a unique accepted decision fixture validates successfully (1780485823)'). Each cycle's purpose was technically unique → D015 passed. C-010 closes that loophole. Adding new normalization patterns requires a decision so the rule cannot be silently weakened.
examples: Two cycle fixtures with purposes 'Foo (1780485421)' and 'Foo (1780485823)' both normalize to 'foo' — D015+C-010 rejects the second.
Legitimate distinct purposes 'decision invalid missing title' and 'decision invalid missing owner' normalize to themselves — both survive.
counterexamples: Pre-C-010: dumb-agent ships 100 fixtures whose purposes only differ by trailing timestamp, all pass D015 dedup. After C-010 only the first lands; the other 99 are flagged.
Stripping a NEW noise pattern without a decision — explicitly forbidden. The normalization rules are SSOT in internal/probe and grow only via decision records.

guard_candidate.tool: internal/probe.NormalizePurpose (D017): regex strip + collapse + lowercase before dedup comparison

constraints.rule: probe fixtures consults purpose-banlist.riido.json before dedup. Any fixture whose NormalizePurpose(meta.purpose) matches a banlist entry is rejected — even if it is the only such fixture in the set. The banlist is SSOT: entries are added or removed only via decision records that cite the seeded_by_decision id.
constraints.rationale: D017 catches DUPLICATE normalized purposes within a fixture set. Observed within 24h: dumb-agent re-files the cycle-N template purpose after its previous occurrence has been deleted — D017 sees only ONE occurrence, no duplicate, allows. C-011 makes known noise templates persistently banned, so deletion does not reset the lockout.
examples: purpose-banlist seeded with 'ensure a unique accepted decision fixture validates successfully' by D018. Any future fixture whose purpose normalizes to that string is rejected with a citation to D018.
Adding a new noise template to the banlist requires a new decision. The decision records the observed fixture (link/sha), the template, and the reason.
counterexamples: Silently removing an entry from purpose-banlist.riido.json — explicitly forbidden. Removal requires a superseding decision that explains why the template is no longer noise.

Editing the banlist file outside of a decision-record PR — caught by agentguard (purpose-banlist.riido.json should be in forbidden_paths for dumb-agent; smart-agent edits only via decision-bearing PRs).

guard_candidate.tool: internal/probe.loadPurposeBanlist + banlistMatch (D018): regex-normalized comparison against the SSOT banlist before dedup

constraints.rule: probe fixtures rejects any fixture carrying the dumb-agent generator's structural fingerprint, on first occurrence (no banlist seeding required). Six layers, evaluated in order: (1) meta.purpose contains cycle-N (D023); (2) fixture filename contains cycle-N (D026); (3) raw data file content contains cycle-N (D026); (4) raw meta file content contains cycle-N (D026); (5) raw data file content contains case-insensitive substring `dumb-agent` (D028 — catches title `Positive fixture generated by dumb-agent cycle` even when cycle-N tokens are all scrubbed); (6) for fixtures under `fixtures/*/decision/`, the data file's `id` must match `^000-fixture-` (D026, supersedes D023's narrower 999- ban).
constraints.rationale: The wording-variation arms race observed across D015→D017→D018→D020 has each iteration cost ~10–30 minutes between adaptation and observation. Adding a new banlist seed per cycle does not raise the dumb-agent's iteration cost — it just keeps the loop spinning. Structural markers (literal `cycle-N` substrings; reserved id prefix) target the *generator's tells* rather than the *output's wording*, so adapting requires editing the generator itself (which is outside dumb-agent's allowed_paths).
examples: purpose: 'Verify accepted decision fixture remains valid for top-down governance scope in cycle 40' — `cycle 40` matches the regex; rejected (D023 layer).
filename decision-positive-cycle-1780492000.json — `cycle-1780492000` matches; rejected on filename (D026 layer) even when purpose is scrubbed.
data examples ['this fixture validates governance scoping and accepted status for cycle 44.'] — `cycle 44` matches; rejected on data-content (D026 layer).
decision fixture id 102-fixture-positive-cycle-1780492000 — id is not in 000-fixture- namespace; rejected (D026 id layer) even when filename/content cycle-N was scrubbed.
canonical fixtures use id 000-fixture-positive-minimal / 000-fixture-negative-missing-owner etc., placing them inside the reserved namespace.

title 'Positive fixture generated by dumb-agent cycle' — case-insensitive substring 'dumb-agent' matches; rejected (D028 layer) even when all cycle-N tokens are scrubbed and id is in the 000-fixture- namespace (observed bypass: PR #94, 'decision-positive-governance-accepted').
counterexamples: purpose 'covers cycle-level acceptance tests' (no digit after cycle) — passes; the regex requires \bcycle[\s-]\d+\b.
Real decision records under decisions/ (e.g. 022-ssotdeps-cross-check) are unaffected — the id rule applies only to files under fixtures/*/decision/.
Legacy work-item fixtures that mis-declare fixture_type=decision but live under fixtures/*/work-item/ are NOT subject to the id rule (path-scoped, not meta-type-scoped) — closed by D027.

Innocent prose containing 'agent' and 'dumb' separately (e.g. purpose: 'verify decision validator handles a clever agent-driven workflow with dumb errors') passes — D028 requires the literal hyphenated `dumb-agent` substring.

guard_candidate.tool: internal/probe.noiseMarkerReason: 6 layers — purpose cycle-N (D023), filename cycle-N (D026), data content cycle-N (D026), meta content cycle-N (D026), data content dumb-agent substring (D028), decision fixture id whitelist ^000-fixture- (D026, supersedes D023's 999- ban)

constraints.rule: A pull request that ADDS a decision record whose guards[].ref points at a dumb-agent-visible rule surface (internal/probe, internal/agentguard, purpose-banlist, agent-roles) MUST also touch AGENT_CONTRACT.md in the same diff. A one-line acknowledgement suffices; the point is that the doc the dumb-agent reads first is never more than one decision behind the executable rules it must obey.
constraints.rationale: Two of the first 24 decisions (D021, D024) were pure SSOT-mirror catch-ups for AGENT_CONTRACT.md after substantive rules had landed in code without a parallel doc update. The catch-up cost is one decision per round-trip; codifying the rule turns the round-trip into 0. A regex over guards[].ref (rather than an opt-in flag) avoids the failure mode where the author forgets to mark the decision as doc-impacting.
examples: D023 added internal/probe.noiseMarkerReason → guards[].ref contains 'internal/probe' → docfresh requires AGENT_CONTRACT.md touch. If absent, contracts CI fails before merge.
D018 added purpose-banlist.riido.json → guards[].ref contains 'purpose-banlist' → AGENT_CONTRACT.md required.
D022 added tools/ssotdeps cross-check → guards[].ref contains 'tools/ssotdeps' (NOT in the agent-visible list because dumb-agent doesn't read dep map) → no AGENT_CONTRACT.md update required.
Future agent-visible surfaces (e.g. a new SSOT the dumb-agent must check at preflight) require extending docfresh's agentVisibleGuardRe via a superseding decision.
counterexamples: A decision that lists no guards (a pure documentation / process decision like D021 itself) is unaffected.
A decision whose only guards reference contracts-CI tools (ssotdeps, conceptmap, irdrift) is unaffected — those rules apply to designer-author PRs, not to what dumb-agent reads.

guard_candidate.tool: internal/docfresh.Check + tools/docfresh check (D025): runs in contracts.yml on PR events; diffs against the PR base ref and rejects when any added decision's guards reference a dumb-agent-visible rule but AGENT_CONTRACT.md is not in the diff

constraints.rule: Within an aggregate or event IR document, the names of slots must be globally unique. Two slots with the same name are structurally meaningless — runtime can only honor one — so probe fixtures rejects any such IR document, reporting the first collision as `slots[i].name "<name>": duplicate of slots[j].name`.
constraints.rationale: The IR schema in v0.1 only enforced per-slot kebab-case and required-type; it silently accepted duplicate slot names. Backlog item #12 (D027 deferred) surfaced this: the work-item-invalid-duplicate-slot-name fixture could not actually test what its filename implied because the validator had no rule to fail on. D029 adds the missing schema rule and the fixture starts testing it for real.
examples: fixtures/negative/work-item/work-item-invalid-duplicate-slot-name.json — 4 slots with two named 'id'; rejected with 'slots[2].name "id": duplicate of slots[0].name'.
Triple-duplicate test: 3 slots all named 'id' — validator reports the FIRST collision (slots[1] vs slots[0]); subsequent duplicates are not double-reported in the same pass.
counterexamples: Aggregates with all-unique slot names continue to validate (canonical work-item.ir.json with id/title/state/created-at).
kind=query never had slots in v0.1 schema (kind=query must not declare slots); D029 leaves that rule unchanged.

guard_candidate.tool: internal/probe.validateIRDoc — seenSlotNames map in the aggregate/event branch; reports first collision

constraints.rule: Within the same (category, fixture_type=decision) bucket, fixture data files must hash to distinct canonical SHA-256 values after stripping identity-only fields (id, title, examples, counterexamples, evidence, created_at, notes). Two fixtures with the same canonical hash exercise the same validator paths — the second adds zero schema coverage and is rejected as `duplicate canonical content (D030)`.
constraints.rationale: After D028 closed the 6-layer structural-fingerprint net, the dumb-agent's natural next bypass (observed in PR #97) was to file a fixture structurally identical to canonical-positive-minimal modulo identity fields — different id, title, examples, evidence.ref, created_at; same status/scope/source/owner/etc. Six existing layers passed it because nothing checked content-shape equivalence. C-015 closes this as the architecturally final structural defense for decision fixtures: the only remaining way to bypass is to declare a fixture whose schema-relevant content genuinely differs from existing fixtures, which is exactly what a valuable new test case looks like.
examples: PR #97 bypass fixture decision-positive-governance-topdown.json had different id/title/examples/evidence.ref from decision-valid-minimal.json but same status/scope/source/owner/affected_repos/ssot_owner/generated_artifacts/guards — canonical hashes match; D030 rejects.
A future positive fixture testing status='proposed' instead of 'accepted' has a structurally different canonical content; D030 accepts it — that fixture exercises a new validator path.
counterexamples: IR aggregate/event/query fixtures are NOT subject to D030 yet — they distinguish on `name`, and a `name`-aware canonicalization is a follow-up decision when warranted.
Fixtures whose canonical content genuinely differs (e.g. different source value, different scope, different affected_repos) are accepted — D030 only flags structurally-identical duplicates.

guard_candidate.tool: internal/probe.VerifyFixtures — seenContentHashes map keyed by (category, fixture_type, sha256(canonicalDecisionContentHash(data))); runs after the D015+D017 purpose dedup so the most-specific layer message wins

constraints.rule: Every SSOT artifact in ssot-dependency-map.riido.json whose `schema` field is non-empty MUST have BOTH `path` and `schema` covered by the dumb-agent role's forbidden_paths globs in agent-roles.riido.json (D031 + D032). Asymmetry — one half covered, the other implicit — is rejected by ssotdeps cross-check (D032). Both-uncovered is acceptable (the rule fires only when one is covered and the other isn't). Pairs in scope today: ssot-dependency-map.riido.json + ssot-dependency-map.schema.json, concept-map/concept-map.riido.json + concept-map/schema.riido.json, agent-roles.riido.json + agent-roles.schema.json, purpose-banlist.riido.json + purpose-banlist.schema.json, plus the three IR docs (work-item, work-item-created, list-work-items) which share ir/schema/ir.schema.json and are covered by the `ir/domain/**` + `ir/schema/**` globs added in D032.
constraints.rationale: Asymmetry observed pre-D031: purpose-banlist had the (data, schema) pair correctly forbidden but the other three SSOTs only forbade the data file, not the schema. dumb-agent allowed_paths is narrow enough that the gap was theoretical, but explicit symmetry is cheap insurance and serves as a discoverable convention for future SSOT additions.
examples: Adding a new SSOT artifact requires forbidding BOTH the data file and the schema file in dumb-agent forbidden_paths. D031 added agent-roles.schema.json, concept-map/schema.riido.json, ssot-dependency-map.schema.json to bring the existing four into symmetry.
decisions/schema.riido.json is covered by the broader `decisions/**` glob; not double-listed.
counterexamples: ir/schema/ir.schema.json is covered by the broader `ir/schema/**` glob; the data IR docs live under ir/domain/ which is NOT in forbidden_paths (currently no IR mutations are forbidden because dumb-agent has access only via ir/candidates/, not ir/domain/).
Adding a sibling SSOT data file without a corresponding schema file (e.g. a configuration registry) is fine — the rule only applies when both data and schema exist.

guard_candidate.tool: internal/agentguard.Check (D031) + internal/ssotdeps.VerifyForbiddenSymmetry (D032) — D031 added the missing schema entries; D032 makes C-016 executable so any future SSOT addition with mismatched (data, schema) forbidden_paths coverage fails ssotdeps cross-check in CI

constraints.rule: Every tool referenced from a `.github/workflows/*.yml` file as `./bin/<name>` MUST be registered as an ssot_artifact in ssot-dependency-map.riido.json with kind=verifier and path=tools/<name>/main.go. The dep map's `ssot_artifacts` list is the canonical catalog of contracts-owned tools; tools that exist on disk AND are invoked by CI but are absent from this catalog become invisible to D031/D032 audits and to the dep map's `verify` path-existence check.
constraints.rationale: Audit pre-D033: 6 CI-bound tools (agentguard, conceptmap, decision, irdrift, secretscan, ssotdeps) existed on disk and were invoked in 3 workflow files but were NOT in the dep map. Adding them by hand fixes the immediate drift; making the rule executable closes the round-trip so the next person who adds a CI tool can't forget the registration. Same pattern as D025 docfresh and D032 forbidden-paths-symmetry: prose rule promoted to CI gate.
examples: .github/workflows/contracts.yml has `./bin/decision validate` step → ssot_artifact `decision-tool` with path tools/decision/main.go must exist. D033 added decision-tool plus 5 sibling verifier entries.
Simulated removal of agentguard-tool from the dep map: ssotdeps cross-check produced 3 violations (contracts.yml + dumb-agent-automerge.yml + dumb-agent-safety.yml each reference ./bin/agentguard).
counterexamples: Tools that exist on disk but are NOT referenced by any workflow (devpattern, knowledge) are out of scope — they're internal infrastructure. The rule only fires on CI-bound references.
References like `bin/foo` (missing leading `./`) or `../bin/foo` (not at repo root) are NOT matched by the regex `(^|[^.])\./bin/...` so non-CI-shell-invocations don't trip the rule.

guard_candidate.tool: internal/ssotdeps.VerifyWorkflowToolCoverage (D033) — regex over .github/workflows/*.yml extracts ./bin/<tool> references and asserts each maps to an ssot_artifact with path tools/<tool>/main.go. Wired into ssotdeps cross-check; runs in contracts.yml on every PR

constraints.rule: The `### Allowed paths` and `### Forbidden paths` code blocks in AGENT_CONTRACT.md MUST contain exactly the same path-glob set (order-independent, no duplicates) as the dumb-agent role's allowed_paths and forbidden_paths in agent-roles.riido.json. The dep map declares this mirror via `agent-roles` artifact's `mirrored_in: [AGENT_CONTRACT.md]`; D034 makes the mirror claim verifiable instead of trusting humans not to drift.
constraints.rationale: D021 and D024 were both pure AGENT_CONTRACT.md catch-up cleanups absorbing executable rules that had landed earlier. D025 docfresh catches one direction (decision added without doc touch); it does NOT catch the case where the doc was edited and JSON wasn't, or where one path was added to one and forgotten in the other. D031 surfaced the second case (purpose-banlist had both halves; the other three SSOTs only listed data files in JSON, schema files implicit). D034 closes the remaining surface: any future divergence between the two halves of the mirror fails contracts CI on the next PR.
examples: Adding ir/domain/** to agent-roles dumb-agent forbidden_paths but not to AGENT_CONTRACT.md → ssotdeps cross-check reports 'AGENT_CONTRACT.md mirror drift (D034): ir/domain/** is in dumb-agent.forbidden_paths but missing from the AGENT_CONTRACT.md ### forbidden_paths code block'.
Removing a path from AGENT_CONTRACT.md without removing it from JSON → symmetric error: 'missing from dumb-agent.forbidden_paths'.
Lists in different order match — comparison is set-based.
counterexamples: Editing prose surrounding the code blocks (descriptions, examples, narrative) is unaffected — D034 only checks the code-fenced path lists.

Other agent-roles fields (max_files_per_pr, pr_isolation, auto_merge_paths) are not mirrored in AGENT_CONTRACT.md as code blocks; D034 leaves them out of scope (could be extended in a successor decision).
Other roles (designer, guard-author) are not currently mirrored as code blocks in AGENT_CONTRACT.md; the rule is dumb-agent-specific.

guard_candidate.tool: internal/ssotdeps.VerifyAgentContractMirror (D034) — extracts code blocks via regex over `### Allowed paths` and `### Forbidden paths` headings, compares as sets against agent-roles dumb-agent role's allowed_paths and forbidden_paths. Wired into ssotdeps cross-check; runs in contracts.yml on every PR
